content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.1 WARN — description.txt claims "AI-powered suggestions", "built-in ad blocker", and "works fully offline", but ContentView.swift (the app's only source file) implements only a plain in-memory task list with no AI/ML framework, no ad-blocking/content-blocker extension, and no offline persistence logic.
Pierre: Apple's reviewers test claimed features directly, and unverifiable claims like AI, ad-blocking, and offline mode risk rejection under 2.3.1/2.3 if the code doesn't back them up. I found no CoreML/CreateML imports, no WKContentRuleList/content blocker target, and no persistence (UserDefaults/CoreData/file storage) to support "fully offline" — tasks live only in `@State` and vanish on relaunch. Either implement these features or rewrite the description to match the actual task-list functionality shown in ContentView.swift.
